basic_details.job_title: BBMB Apprentice Recruitment 2026 - Apply Online for 71 ITI, Diploma & Graduate Apprentice Posts
basic_details.short_information: Bhakra Beas Management Board (BBMB) has released Advertisement No. 01/2026 inviting online applications for 71 apprentice posts across ITI, Diploma, and Graduate/Degree trades under Bhakra Power House Circle, Nangal, for the 2026–27 training year. Applications opened on 14 July 2026 and will close on 10 August 2026. Selection is based purely on merit - there is no written exam and no interview.

age_limit.details: Age limit is not specified in the notification. It states that age eligibility will be as per the Apprenticeship Act, 1961, and its amendments from time to time.

qualification.details: Passed 10th class examination with minimum 40% marks from a recognized board. Must hold a National Trade Certificate in the notified trade issued by NCVT/State Council for Vocational Training. Must have completed the relevant ITI course. Aadhaar Card is compulsory. | Completed Graduation (or equivalent) in the concerned stream with minimum 40% marks from a recognized institute. Aadhaar Card is compulsory. | Completed Diploma with minimum 40% marks from a recognized institute. Aadhaar Card is compulsory. | Note: There is no interview and no written exam for this recruitment.

selection_process: Graduate 20% to 10th class + 20% to 12th/Diploma (whichever higher, in case of both) + 60% to aggregate in Engineering/Graduation/PG (whichever applicable) in relevant subject 100
Diploma 30% to 10th class + 70% to Diploma in relevant field 100
ITI 30% to 10th class + 70% to ITI in concerned trade 100
Eligible candidates are selected from the NAPS/NATS apprenticeship portal database. A merit list is prepared for all applicants based on percentage of marks

25% of total available seats per trade are reserved for candidates holding a residency certificate of the districts where the Project/Power Station/Unit is located. If these reserved seats remain unfilled
they go to candidates with a residency certificate of the concerned State. Remaining seats are filled through open merit. Project areas covered: Bhakra & Nangal Dam Project - Ropar
and Una. In case two candidates have the same marks
the older candidate is preferred. If date of birth is also the same
the candidate who passed matriculation earlier is considered first.
how_to_apply: ITI trade candidates should apply online through the National Apprenticeship Promotion Scheme (NAPS) portal at apprenticeshipindia.gov.in.
Graduate, Degree, and Diploma candidates should apply online through the NATS portal at nats.education.gov.in.
Applications must be submitted online between 14 July 2026 and 10 August 2026.
No other mode of application will be accepted.
important_instructions: Candidates whose results are still awaited on the last date of receipt of applications, for the qualifying examinations mentioned in the eligibility criteria, will not be considered. Selection and deployment of apprentices will be subject to detailed provisions of the Apprenticeship Act, 1961, and its time-to-time amendments. Results will be uploaded on the BBMB website through the Office of the System Software Manager, BBMB, Chandigarh. Candidates who have already undergone apprenticeship training (completed or ongoing) under the Apprenticeship Act, 1961, in any other establishment are not eligible to apply. Selected candidates will be registered under the NAPS/NATS Portal. Candidates already registered and trained under these schemes will not be entertained and need not apply. Incomplete applications, in any manner, will be rejected without assigning any reason. The date and time for document verification will be decided by the office. Candidates found absent at the time of verification will have their application rejected without assigning any reason.

rows.details: Bhakra Power House Circle, BBMB Nangal

content_sections.heading: BBMB Apprentice Vacancy Details
content_sections.text: A total of 71 apprentice posts have been announced across Degree, Diploma, and ITI categories. The notification states 71 posts in total. The combined trade-wise figures above (excluding Painter General and Computer Operator and Programming Assistant) sum to 60 posts, leaving 11 posts to be split between these two trades. The source document is not clearly legible for these two exact figures - please verify against the original notification before publishing (see Flag for Sila).

rows.no_of_posts: Not confirmed - printed value unclear in source; see notes below
Not confirmed - printed value unclear in source; see notes below

content_sections.heading: BBMB Apprentice Eligibility Criteria
content_sections.text: Passed 10th class examination with minimum 40% marks from a recognized board. Must hold a National Trade Certificate in the notified trade issued by NCVT/State Council for Vocational Training. Must have completed the relevant ITI course. Aadhaar Card is compulsory. Completed Graduation (or equivalent) in the concerned stream with minimum 40% marks from a recognized institute. Aadhaar Card is compulsory. Completed Diploma with minimum 40% marks from a recognized institute. Aadhaar Card is compulsory. Note: There is no interview and no written exam for this recruitment.

content_sections.heading: BBMB Apprentice Age Limit
content_sections.text: Age limit is not specified in the notification. It states that age eligibility will be as per the Apprenticeship Act, 1961, and its amendments from time to time.

content_sections.heading: BBMB Apprentice Selection Process
content_sections.text: Eligible candidates are selected from the NAPS/NATS apprenticeship portal database. A merit list is prepared for all applicants based on percentage of marks, with category-specific weightage as below. 25% of total available seats per trade are reserved for candidates holding a residency certificate of the districts where the Project/Power Station/Unit is located. If these reserved seats remain unfilled, they go to candidates with a residency certificate of the concerned State. Remaining seats are filled through open merit. Project areas covered: Bhakra & Nangal Dam Project - Ropar, Bilaspur, and Una. In case two candidates have the same marks, the older candidate is preferred. If date of birth is also the same, the candidate who passed matriculation earlier is considered first.

rows.weightage: 20% to 10th class + 20% to 12th/Diploma (whichever higher, in case of both) + 60% to aggregate in Engineering/Graduation/PG (whichever applicable) in relevant subject
30% to 10th class + 70% to Diploma in relevant field
30% to 10th class + 70% to ITI in concerned trade

content_sections.heading: BBMB Apprentice Reservation
content_sections.text: Reservation will be as per the Apprentice Act, 1961, and its amendments from time to time.

content_sections.heading: Documents Required for BBMB Apprentice Verification
content_sections.text: 10th Mark sheet ITI Certificate and Marksheets (must be issued by NCVT/SCVT) Category certificate (SC/ST/OBC, if applicable) Residence/Domicile certificate (if applicable) Aadhaar Card

content_sections.heading: How to Apply for BBMB Apprentice Recruitment 2026
content_sections.text: ITI trade candidates should apply online through the National Apprenticeship Promotion Scheme (NAPS) portal at apprenticeshipindia.gov.in. Graduate, Degree, and Diploma candidates should apply online through the NATS portal at nats.education.gov.in. Applications must be submitted online between 14 July 2026 and 10 August 2026. No other mode of application will be accepted.

content_sections.heading: Important Instructions
content_sections.text: Candidates whose results are still awaited on the last date of receipt of applications, for the qualifying examinations mentioned in the eligibility criteria, will not be considered. Selection and deployment of apprentices will be subject to detailed provisions of the Apprenticeship Act, 1961, and its time-to-time amendments. Results will be uploaded on the BBMB website through the Office of the System Software Manager, BBMB, Chandigarh. Candidates who have already undergone apprenticeship training (completed or ongoing) under the Apprenticeship Act, 1961, in any other establishment are not eligible to apply. Selected candidates will be registered under the NAPS/NATS Portal. Candidates already registered and trained under these schemes will not be entertained and need not apply. Incomplete applications, in any manner, will be rejected without assigning any reason. The date and time for document verification will be decided by the office. Candidates found absent at the time of verification will have their application rejected without assigning any reason.

faq.question: Q1. What is the last date to apply for BBMB Apprentice Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The last date to apply online is 10 August 2026.

faq.question: Q2. How many vacancies are there in BBMB Apprentice Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: There are 71 apprentice posts across ITI, Diploma, and Graduate/Degree trades.

faq.question: Q3. Where can I apply for BBMB Apprentice Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: ITI trade candidates apply on the NAPS portal (apprenticeshipindia.gov.in); Graduate, Degree, and Diploma candidates apply on the NATS portal (nats.education.gov.in).

faq.question: Q4. Is there a written exam or interview for BBMB Apprentice Recruitment?
faq.answer: No. There is no written exam and no interview. Selection is based on a merit list prepared from academic percentages.

faq.question: Q5. What is the age limit for BBMB Apprentice Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The notification does not specify an age limit; it states that age eligibility follows the Apprenticeship Act, 1961, and its amendments.

faq.question: Q6. Is Aadhaar Card compulsory for BBMB Apprentice Recruitment?
faq.answer: Yes, an Aadhaar Card is compulsory for all categories of applicants.

faq.question: What is the last date to apply for BBMB Apprentice Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The last date to apply online is 10 August 2026.
